text.title: SDD Workflows Navigation Hub Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every workflows (navigation hub) application MUST enforce, plus a skip-this-methodology fallback. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: This methodology MUST emit a route record only — it MUST NOT author spec / design / plan content. Authoring is the responsibility of `workflow-spec-phase`, `workflow-design-phase`, and `writing-implementation-plans`. A route record that contains spec/design/plan body content is a violation.
rule.rationale: Mixing routing with authoring creates duplication and conflicts when the authoring methodologies evolve. The router stays small and stable; the authors carry the heavy logic.

rule.statement: Phase computation MUST read `status` fields from doc frontmatter (yaml). The router MUST NOT infer phase from filename or directory location alone — those are unreliable signals. Missing frontmatter is treated as `Draft`.
rule.rationale: Filenames lie; renames happen; directory states lag. Frontmatter is the contract. Reading it directly is the only reliable signal.

rule.statement: Each feature has EXACTLY ONE active phase at a time. The active phase is the earliest phase whose required input doc is not yet Accepted. If spec Accepted + design Draft → active phase is Design. If spec Draft → active phase is Spec regardless of what else exists.
rule.rationale: Concurrent-phase authoring is the leading cause of spec-design drift. The "earliest-non-Accepted-input" rule is the simple invariant that prevents it.

rule.statement: If the route cannot resolve (e.g., no feature directory, malformed docs, conflicting status), the route record MUST name the specific blocker. Vague messages ("cannot route", "error") are forbidden — they force the caller to re-investigate.
rule.rationale: Specific blockers cut median time-to-unblock by 3-5x because the caller can act without re-discovery.

rule.statement: The `next_methodology` field MUST be a real path under `skills/faion/knowledge/<tier>/<group>/<slug>/` that exists on disk. Placeholder strings or empty values are forbidden — the caller will dispatch this path directly.
rule.rationale: Stub paths produce immediate dispatch failure. A pre-validated path keeps the orchestration loop unbroken.

rule.statement: If any Applies-If precondition fails OR any Skip-If condition triggers, do NOT apply this methodology. Emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition / triggered skip, and route the task to the correct methodology (or none).
rule.rationale: Methodology hygiene: mis-application produces worse artefacts than no methodology because downstream consumers trust the schema; the skip-record makes non-application traceable.
